title: Electroencephalographic responses to the number of objects in partially occluded and uncovered scenes
description: This dataset contains electroencephalographic (EEG) recordings from 23 participants performing a passive viewing task involving visual scenes with occluded and uncovered game pieces. Participants viewed scenes with varying numbers of visible and hidden objects to investigate neural responses to object numerosity under occlusion conditions. The study employed a factorial design with 640 trials across eight blocks, systematically manipulating the number of initially visible pieces (4 or 32) and uncovered pieces (0-32) to examine how the brain processes object quantity in partially occluded visual scenes.
methods_description: Participants performed a passive viewing task in a dimly lit room. Each trial consisted of a 1-second fixation cross with drift correction, followed by a 2-second presentation of a game board with occluder (game pieces appearing in the final 1 second), and a 1-second uncovered scene phase. The experiment comprised eight blocks of 80 trials each (640 total trials) with a 2×8 factorial design: 2 levels of initially visible pieces (4 or 32) crossed with 8 levels of uncovered pieces (0, 1, 2, 4, 28, 30, 31, or 32). Each condition was repeated 40 times.

funding_references.award_title: ERC Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme

funding_references.funder_name: Hessian Ministry of Higher Education, Science, Research and Art
